title: Elasticsearch Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Elasticsearch** is the canonical distributed search + analytics engine. This connector — `ElasticsearchConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "elasticsearch"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from a cluster:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Cluster | `/`, `/_cluster/health` | Liveness + green/yellow/red health |
| Indices | `/_cat/indices`, `/{index}` | List, get, create, delete |
| Documents | `/{index}/_doc/{id}` | Index / get / update / delete |
| Search | `/{index}/_search`, `/{index}/_count` | Full DSL + count |
| Bulk | `/_bulk` | NDJSON paired action+source |
| Mapping | `/{index}/_mapping` | Get + put mappings |
| Aliases | `/_cat/aliases` | List aliases |
| Snapshots | `/_snapshot/{repo}/_all` | Snapshot inventory |

**Per-tenant cluster URL.** Unlike SaaS connectors with a single fixed host, every Elasticsearch deployment carries its own `base_url` (Elastic Cloud → `https://*.cloud.es.io:9243`, self-hosted → operator-controlled). The connector reads `self.config["base_url"]` (alias accepted: `host`) and never embeds a default.

The connector normalises one `NormalizedDocument` per index (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`) on sync, surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::ElasticsearchHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate an API key

**Elastic Cloud / Kibana:**
1. Sign in to Kibana for your deployment.
2. **Stack Management → Security → API Keys → Create API Key**.
3. Name the key, optionally grant cluster + index privileges, click **Create**.
4. Kibana shows the **encoded** key once — copy it (format: `base64(id:api_key)`).
5. Copy your **Cluster URL** from **Deployments → Manage → Copy endpoint** (Elasticsearch endpoint, not Kibana). Format: `https://<deployment-id>.es.<region>.aws.elastic-cloud.com:9243`.

**Self-hosted (no API key needed for anonymous):**
1. The cluster URL is your Elasticsearch listener — typically `https://es.internal:9200`.
2. For credentials, either generate an API key (`POST /_security/api_key`) or use HTTP Basic with an existing user.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Elasticsearch**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `base_url` (string, required) | Cluster URL from step 5 |
| `api_key` (secret) | Encoded API key (preferred) |
| `username` + `password` | HTTP Basic fallback (omit when api_key is set) |
| `verify_ssl` (boolean, default true) | Disable for self-signed dev only |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number, default 600) | Soft client-side cap |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `base_url` is present, builds the HTTP client, probes `GET /` to verify reachability + credentials, and persists config.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which probes `GET /_cluster/health`. On green → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On yellow → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED` (yellow is operational, just unreplicated). On red → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED`. On 401 → `DEGRADED + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On unreachable → `OFFLINE + CONNECTED` with the network error.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Elasticsearch supports three auth surfaces; this connector exposes the two server-to-server ones plus anonymous.

### API key (preferred) — `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`

- Generated from **Kibana → Stack Management → API Keys**.
- Kibana returns an *encoded* key (`base64(id:api_key)`).
- Sent as `Authorization: ApiKey <encoded>`. **No `Bearer` prefix** — Elasticsearch-specific gotcha.

### HTTP Basic (fallback)

- `username` + `password` install fields.
- Sent as `Authorization: Basic <base64(username:password)>`.
- Useful for self-hosted clusters where API keys aren't enabled.
- API key wins when both are configured.

### Anonymous (rare)

- Self-hosted dev clusters with `xpack.security.enabled: false` accept unauthenticated requests.
- Leave `api_key`, `username`, `password` all blank — the `Authorization` header is omitted.
- **Never** acceptable for production. Always audit before enabling.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `base_url`. Builds `ElasticsearchHTTPClient`. Probes `GET /`. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a `TokenInfo` whose access_token is the configured `api_key` (ABI shim — no real exchange). |
| `health_check()` | Probes `GET /_cluster/health` — returns even on yellow/red. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — no token to refresh. |
| token storage | None — credentials live in `self.config` only. |

### Header contract

```
Authorization: ApiKey <encoded>     ← api_key path
            OR Basic  <base64(u:p)> ← basic path
            OR (absent)             ← anonymous
Content-Type: application/json      ← all but /_bulk
            OR application/x-ndjson ← /_bulk only
Accept:       application/json
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `base_url` | string | **yes** | `self.base_url` | Per-tenant cluster URL (`https://*.cloud.es.io:9243` or self-hosted). Legacy alias `host` is accepted. |
| `api_key` | secret | no | `self.api_key` | Encoded Kibana API key — `Authorization: ApiKey` value |
| `username` | string | no | `self.username` | HTTP Basic fallback |
| `password` | secret | no | `self.password` | Required with `username` |
| `verify_ssl` | boolean | no (default true) | `self.verify_ssl` | TLS verification — never disable in prod |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no (default 600) | `self.rate_limit_per_min` | Soft client-side cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["base_url"]
OPTIONAL_CONFIG_KEYS = [
    "api_key", "username", "password", "verify_ssl", "rate_limit_per_min",
]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
```

### Why `api_key` is optional

For SaaS connectors `api_key` is mandatory. Elasticsearch is different: anonymous self-hosted clusters exist and are legitimate for internal CI / dev fixtures. The install gate is `base_url` only — if no credentials are supplied, the connector falls through to anonymous mode and the cluster decides (401 → `INVALID_CREDENTIALS`, 200 → `CONNECTED`).

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `ElasticsearchConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::ElasticsearchHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `base_url`; build `ElasticsearchHTTPClient`; probe `GET /` to verify reachability + credentials; persist config via `save_config()`.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth — returns `TokenInfo(access_token=api_key, token_type="api_key")` for ABI compatibility.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Probes `GET /_cluster/health`. Green → HEALTHY. Yellow → HEALTHY (operational, unreplicated). Red → DEGRADED. 401 → INVALID_CREDENTIALS. 429 → DEGRADED. Transport error → OFFLINE.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Enumerates `/_cat/indices/*?format=json`; for each row, builds a `NormalizedDocument(id=f"{tenant_id}_{index_name}")` describing health, docs.count, store.size; calls `ingest_document()`. Returns `SyncResult(documents_found, documents_synced, documents_failed)`.

children.title: Cluster
children.content:
### `async get_cluster_health(level="cluster") -> Dict`
`GET /_cluster/health?level={level}`. level ∈ `cluster | indices | shards`. Returns the full payload — `cluster_name`, `status`, `number_of_nodes`, `active_primary_shards`, etc.

### `async cluster_health(level="cluster") -> Dict`
Back-compat shim → `get_cluster_health()`. Kept for older callers.

children.title: Indices
children.content:
### `async list_indices(index_pattern="*", format="json") -> List | Any`
`GET /_cat/indices/{pattern}?format={fmt}`. Returns the array of `/_cat`-style rows: `index, health, status, uuid, pri, rep, docs.count, docs.deleted, store.size`.

### `async get_index(index) -> Dict`
`GET /{index}` — full index info envelope `{index: {settings, mappings, aliases}}`.

### `async create_index(index, settings=None, mappings=None) -> Dict`
`PUT /{index}` — body: `{settings, mappings}` (both optional). Returns `{acknowledged, shards_acknowledged, index}`.

### `async delete_index(index) -> Dict`
`DELETE /{index}` — irreversible. Returns `{acknowledged: True}`.

children.title: Documents
children.content:
### `async index_document(index, document, doc_id=None, refresh="false") -> Dict`
`POST /{index}/_doc` (auto-id) or `PUT /{index}/_doc/{id}` (upsert when `doc_id` provided). `refresh` ∈ `true | false | wait_for`.

### `async get_document(index, doc_id) -> Dict`
`GET /{index}/_doc/{id}`. Returns `{_index, _id, _version, found, _source: {...}}`. Raises `ElasticsearchNotFound` on 404.

### `async update_document(index, doc_id, doc, doc_as_upsert=False) -> Dict`
`POST /{index}/_update/{id}` — body: `{doc: {...}, doc_as_upsert?: True}`. Partial update + optional upsert.

### `async delete_document(index, doc_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /{index}/_doc/{id}`. Returns `{_index, _id, result: "deleted"}`.

children.title: Search · Count · Bulk
children.content:
### `async search(index, query, size=10, from_=0, sort=None, aggs=None) -> Dict`
`POST /{index}/_search` — body: `{query, size, from, sort?, aggs?}`. Full DSL.

### `async count(index, query=None) -> Dict`
`POST /{index}/_count` — body: `{query?: {...}}`. Returns `{count, _shards}`.

### `async bulk(operations) -> Dict`
`POST /_bulk` with `Content-Type: application/x-ndjson`. The *operations* list is the **already-paired** action+source list (e.g. `[{"index": {"_index": "x"}}, {"field": "v"}]`); the client serialises to NDJSON, terminates with `\n`, and ships. The caller owns the pairing semantics — index, create, update, delete actions can all be mixed.

children.title: Mapping · Aliases · Snapshots
children.content:
### `async get_mapping(index) -> Dict`
`GET /{index}/_mapping`. Returns `{<index>: {mappings: {properties: {...}}}}`.

### `async put_mapping(index, properties) -> Dict`
`PUT /{index}/_mapping` — body: `{properties: {...}}`. Add or update field mappings.

### `async list_aliases(name="*") -> List | Any`
`GET /_cat/aliases[/{name}]?format=json`. Returns `[{alias, index, filter, routing.index, routing.search}]`. When `name="*"`, hits `/_cat/aliases` for the cluster-wide list.

### `async list_snapshots(repository) -> Dict`
`GET /_snapshot/{repository}/_all`. Returns `{snapshots: [{snapshot, state, start_time, ...}]}`. Useful for backup inventory.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
ElasticsearchError                       # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── ElasticsearchAuthError                # 401 / 403
├── ElasticsearchNotFound                 # 404 — index_not_found / doc missing
├── ElasticsearchRateLimitError           # 429 — circuit breaker / search-rate cap
└── ElasticsearchNetworkError             # 5xx / transport errors
```

### Retry behaviour (`helpers/utils.py::with_retry`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 | Raise immediately — caller-fixable |
| 429 | Exponential backoff `RETRY_DELAY_S * BACKOFF_FACTOR ** attempt` + jitter, retry up to `max_retries=3` |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TransportError` / `httpx.TimeoutException` | Mapped to `ElasticsearchNetworkError`, retried via `with_retry` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` maps exceptions through `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)         — logged as warning
5xx → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,  CONNECTED)
green / yellow → ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY,  CONNECTED)
red            → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

### Wire-level Elasticsearch error envelope

```json
{
  "error": {
    "type": "illegal_argument_exception",
    "reason": "unknown field [bogus]",
    "root_cause": [ ... ]
  },
  "status": 400
}
```

The connector reads `error.reason` (preferred) → `error.type` → falls back to the raw envelope when neither is present.

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `base_url` blank | Re-enter the cluster URL in ACP. For Elastic Cloud, copy the **Elasticsearch** endpoint (NOT the Kibana endpoint). |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at install/health | 401 — key revoked or wrong format | Verify the key was copied **encoded** (Kibana shows it once). Regenerate from Kibana → API Keys if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` | 403 — key authenticates but lacks privilege | Edit the API key in Kibana → Security → API Keys → assign cluster + index privileges (`monitor`, `read`, `write`, `manage_index`). |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, CONNECTED)` at health | Transport error — DNS, TLS, firewall | `curl -sS <base_url>/_cluster/health` from the connector host. For self-signed clusters, set `verify_ssl=false` in ACP (dev only). |
| `elasticsearch.rate_limit — retrying` warnings spike | 429 — search-rate / circuit breaker | Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or batch fewer bulk operations. Check Kibana → Monitoring for `breaker.parent.tripped`. |
| `ElasticsearchNotFound` on every `get_document` | Wrong index name OR document never indexed | `list_indices()` to confirm the index exists; `count(index)` to confirm docs are present. |
| `bulk()` returns `errors: true` but call doesn't raise | Per-row failures (e.g. mapping conflict) | Iterate `response["items"]` — each entry has `index.status` and `index.error` for the failing rows. |
| `400 illegal_argument_exception` on `search` | Malformed DSL — wrong field name, wrong query type | Read `error.reason` from the raised `ElasticsearchError`. Common: querying a `keyword` field with `match` (use `term`) or vice versa. |
| `health_check()` returns DEGRADED red | One or more primary shards unassigned | `get_cluster_health(level="indices")` shows per-index status. Typically a node is down or disk-watermark is high. |
| `list_snapshots(repository)` returns 404 | Snapshot repository not registered | `PUT /_snapshot/{repo}` via Kibana → Snapshot and Restore → Repositories. |
| 401 on anonymous mode | Cluster has `xpack.security.enabled: true` | Generate an API key or set `username`+`password`. Anonymous is only valid when security is explicitly disabled cluster-side. |
